creator: bugs-freebsd-org916@ketas.si.pri.ee
text: i could, but if problem is so complex like that, bumping abseil won't help either? basically, as i get it, if abseil is compiled with certain clang and clang is upgraded in between, how does anybody know when to bump what? any time the clang is touched in base/ports? i don't know. the problem with testing is that it's same compiler and so it works. if you just built abseil you got fresh, others get old. yet version is same. i'm not aware if abseil needs whole chain rebuilt downstream as well. so, in the end it affects people who have fresh abseil sitting on disk. no need to upgrade it since it's latest already. nobody will know it's now stale because it came from old clang. i can't even wrap my head around it all. maybe i'll ask abseil maintainer on maybe it start getting bumps and yeah i guess you're correct that you did nothing wrong here. i'll think what/who to contact
